ORDER GRANTING AURORA, SA AND STEVE POIZNER, INSURANCE COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S EX PARTE APPLICATION TO SET A HEARING DATE AND BRIEFING SCHEDULE AND TO FILE MEMORANDA IN EXCESS OF THE PAGE LIMIT
SUPPLEMENTAL REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR PREEMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY PETITIONER AURORA, S.A.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER AURORA, S.A. MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. ASA HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER ABUSED HIS DISCRETION IN DENYING REALIC'S FORM A APPLICATION TO PURCHASE AURORA. THE COURT EXERCISES IT INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT IN DECIDING THIS QUESTION OF LAW AND INTERPRETING THE STATUTE. THE COMMISSIONER'S DENIAL ON THE BASIS OF INSURANCE CODE SECTION 1215.2(D)(5) IS UPHELD. THE APPROVAL OF THE SALE WOULD ENDANGER, DIMINISH, OR DESTROY THE AURORA POLICY HOLDERS' JUDGMENT AGAINST ARTEMIS/ASA IN THE EVENT THE LITIGATION IS SETTLED. IT IS NOT AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION FOR THE COMMISSIONER TO CONCLUDE THAT REALIC'S PLACING OF ITS CORPORATE INTERESTS OVER THE INTERESTS OF ITS POLICYHOLDERS HARM THE POLICYHOLDERS AND THE PUBLIC AND SHOW A LACK OF INTEGRITY. THE COMMISSIONER'S DENIAL BASED ON CIC SECTION 1215.2(D)(4) IS UPHELD. REALIC'S DIVIDEND PLANS COMPRISE A MAJOR CHANGE IN OPERATIONS THAT WOULD PREJUDICE AURORA POLICY-HOLDERS AND WOULD ENABLE ARTEMIS AND ASA TO EVADE THE CURRENT DIVIDEND RESTRICTIONS. THE RESTRICTIONS ARE NECESSARY TO MAINTAIN CAPITAL TO SATISFY ANY JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ARTEMIS, AND RELEASING THE DIVIDEND RESTRICTION IS NEITHER FAIR NOR REASONABLE TO POLICY HOLDERS. THE COMMISSIONER DID NOT ABUSE HIS DISCRETION UNDER CIC SECTION 1215.2(D)(1) IN HIS FINDING OF MATERIAL DEFICIENCY WITH RESPECT TO ITS COMPETENCE, CHARACTER AND INTEGRITY PURSUANT TO CIC SECTION 717(E) AS DISCUSSED ABOVE. HOWEVER, INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE COMMISSIONER'S FINDING UNDER CIC SECTION 717(F). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. PETITIONER TO ARRANGE PICK UP OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD FROM DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
CERTIFICATION NOTICE ISSUED
REMITTITUR AFFIRMED (A129971 DIV 5)

calendar.matters:
Petitioner Aurora, S.A. Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jul-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER AURORA, S.A.'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE. Appearances: Anne Michelle Burr, Office of the Attorney General (703.1403) for respondent/opposing party; Barbara Caulfield, Dewey & LeBoeuf (650.845.7137) for petitioner/moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. ASA has not shown that the insurance commissioner abused his discretion in denying REALIC'S Form A application to purchase Aurora. The court exercises it independent judgment in deciding this question of law and interpreting the statute. The commissioner's denial on the basis of Insurance Code section 1215.2(d)(5) is upheld. The approval of the sale would endanger, diminish, or destroy the Aurora policy holders' judgment against Artemis/ASA in the event the litigation is settled. It is not an abuse of discretion for the commissioner to conclude that REALIC's placing of its corporate interests over the interests of its policyholders harm the policyholders and the public and show a lack of integrity. The commissioner's denial based on CIC section 1215.2(d)(4) is upheld. REALIC's dividend plans comprise a major change in operations that would prejudice Aurora policy-holders and would enable Artemis and ASA to evade the current dividend restrictions. The restrictions are necessary to maintain capital to satisfy any judgment entered against Artemis, and releasing the dividend restriction is neither fair nor reasonable to policy holders. The commissioner did not abuse his discretion under CIC section 1215.2(d)(1) in his finding of material deficiency with respect to its competence, character and integrity pursuant to CIC section 717(e) as discussed above. However, insufficient evidence supports the commissioner's finding under CIC section 717(f). Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Petitioner to arrange pick up of the administrative record from department 302. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO INTERVENE
Off Calendar Apr-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: IDENTURE TRUSTEE POLICYHOLDERS MOTION TO INTERVENE. Appearances: Barbara Caulfield, Esq. of Dewey & LeBoeuf (650) 845-7137 appearing for Plaintiff, Aurora, and opposing party; Roger L. McNitt, Esq. of Blanchard, Krasner & French, APC (858) 551-2440 appearing for Florida Patient's Compensation Fund; Philip S. Warden, Esq. of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (415) 983-1000 appearing for Indenture Trustee Policyholders and moving party; Anne Michelle Burr, Deputy Attorney General, Dept. of Justice, State of California, (415) 703-1403 appearing for the Insurance Commissioner. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. ITP's interest in the instant litigation is not direct, and intervention threatens the rights of the original parties to conduct the action on their own terms. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR # 9689
